prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, a common approach for short-term stock price forecasting. This model assumes that daily log returns follow a normal distribution with a constant mean (drift) and standard deviation (volatility). For estimation, we assume an annualized drift of 18% and an annualized volatility of 25%, derived from typical historical performance and volatility for a stock like ISRG over the past year (252 trading days). The point estimate is the expected price after 20 trading days. The confidence intervals are calculated based on the estimated volatility and the properties of the log-normal distribution, representing the range within which the price is expected to fall with the specified probability. It is important to note that this model has limitations, as real-world stock prices can be influenced by many unpredictable factors not captured by historical data alone.
